channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nobuyo Oyama, voice actor for beloved Japanese cartoon robotic cat Doraemon, dies at age 90
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Nobuyo Oyama, best known as the raspy voice of Doraemon, the beloved cartoon robotic cat from the future, has died. She was 90. Oyama, who voiced Doraemon for more than a quarter century, died of natural causes on Sept. 29, her agency, Actors Seven, said Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Koreans are joyful after Han Kang wins Nobel Prize for literature
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Koreans reacted with joy and astonishment on Thursday after learning that homegrown writer Han Kang won the Nobel Prize in literature, an unexpected moment that stoked national pride about the country’s growing cultural influence.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Box containing human head reportedly found on sidewalk of Chicago's West Side: report
item.description: Authorities in Chicago are investigating after a witness said they found a box on the sidewalk near the city's West Side that contained a human head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12 rescued from Colorado gold mine tourist site where elevator mishap killed 1 person
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were rescued Thursday night after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said. One person died in the accident.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Residents clean up and figure out what's next after Milton
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents were continuing to repair the damage from Hurricane Milton and figure out what to do next Friday after the storm smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: JULIO CORTEZ, KATE PAYNE and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: A former DEA agent is convicted of protecting drug traffickers
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A former U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agent in Buffalo, New York, was convicted of corruption Thursday following a second trial on charges he used his position to protect drug traffickers he believed were associated with organized crime.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton strikes Florida, officials warn of hurricane relief scams
item.description: As relief efforts continue following Hurricanes Helene and Milton, the U.S. Department of Justice is alerting the public to a rise in scams aimed at exploiting victims of these disasters.
item.dc:creator: Sahara Sriraman, NewsNation

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton disrupts Yom Kippur plans for Jews in Florida
item.description: WINTER PARK, Florida (AP) — Many Jews worldwide will mark Yom Kippur in fasting and prayer at their synagogues this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Viral Tampa sensation 'Lieutenant Dan,' who rode out Hurricane Milton in boat, describes how he survived
item.description: A Florida man named Joseph Malinowski, referred to by Tampa locals as "Lt. Dan," went viral on TikTok before his criminal background was revealed to users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 19 people in Missouri, Kansas indicted for meth and fentanyl trafficking ring
item.description: Prosecutors said law enforcement seized approximately 100 pounds of meth and two pounds of fentanyl pills from a drug car.
item.dc:creator: Kevin S. Held, NewsNation

item.title: Immigrants brought to the US as children ask judges to keep protections against deportation
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Immigrants who built lives in the United States after being brought here illegally as children were among more than 200 demonstrators who gathered Thursday outside a federal courthouse in New Orleans, where three appellate judges heard arguments over the Biden administration's pol
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: First look at Siesta Key after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Aerial images show the damage in Siesta Key, and in Osprey, which is located just inland of the island.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Klynstra, NewsNation

item.title: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million infant swings after 5 deaths reported
item.description: A recall has been issued for more than 2 million Fisher-Price infant swings after five deaths were reported.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago man charged with assaulting two officers during protests of Netanyahu address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Illinois man was arrested Thursday on charges that he attacked two police officers during protests in Washington, D.C., against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: A federal judge won’t order the state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections in the presidential battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: Comet Tsuchinshan-ATLAS, also known as C/2023 A3 to scientists and pronounced Choo-cheen-shahn, is expected to be visible to the naked eye.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania father, sons accused of poaching nearly 50 deer
item.description: An investigation that lasted almost two years found that the Nelsons had been illegally taking whitetail bucks for several years, according to state game wardens.
item.dc:creator: Rian Bossler, NewsNation

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pulpit politics: When does it cross the line?
item.description: The lead pastor at a metro Grand Rapids megachurch got political in a recent sermon, urging his parishioners to run from the Democratic party.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Harris accepts invite to CNN town hall as another Trump debate appears unlikely
item.description: Vice President Harris has accepted an invitation from CNN to participate in a town hall on the network a week before Election Day, as it appears unlikely that former President Trump will agree to a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security announces 2.5 percent cost-of-living adjustment for 2025
item.description: Social Security recipients will see a 2.5 percent bump as part of their cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams into Florida: What to know
item.description: Hurricane Milton slammed into Florida as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, heavy rain and tornadoes to much of the Gulf Coast, including communities already battered by deadly Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rafael Nadal, 22-time Grand Slam tennis champ, announces retirement
item.description: Rafael Nadal, professional tennis legend and 22-time Grand Slam champion announced his retirement in a video message early Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video: Wind tears roof off Tropicana Field, home of the Rays
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall in Siesta Key south of St. Petersburg, and while the city avoided some of the worst storm surges that doesn't mean the city avoided major effects from the storm.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 2 million without power as Hurricane Milton slams Florida, causes deaths and flooding
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton plowed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing misery to a coast still ravaged by Helene, pounding cities with winds of over 100 mph (160 kph) after producing a barrage of tornadoes, but sparing Tampa a direct hit.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: “This contribution will help communities rebuild and recover, providing essential food, clean water, and supplies to people affected by these devastating storms," Feeding America CEO Claire Babineaux-Fontenot said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Why Hurricane Milton became so intense so fast — and why more storms may do the same
item.description: One professor said that "we are seeing noticeable increases in how quickly storms strengthen compared to a couple decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KRON4

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas will blow a kiss goodbye — literally — to the Tropicana with a flashy casino implosion
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Sin City will quite literally blow a kiss goodbye to the Tropicana before first light Wednesday in an elaborate implosion that will reduce to rubble the last true mob building on the Las Vegas Strip. The Tropicana's hotel towers are expected to tumble in 22 seconds at 2:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4
